skill.name: office-productivity
skill.description: Office productivity workflow covering document creation, spreadsheet automation, presentation generation, and integration with LibreOffice and Microsoft Office formats.
skill.summary: Office productivity workflow covering document creation, spreadsheet automation, presentation genera...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 71 static findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation/guidance meta-skill describing how to invoke other office productivity skills. The detected patterns (external_commands, weak crypto, network reconnaissance) are misinterpreted text strings like skill names (e.g., '@libreoffice-writer') and format extensions (e.g., 'docx', 'odf'). No executable code exists in this skill.

high_findings.title: False Positive: External Command Detection
high_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at lines 32-218. These are skill name references (e.g., '@libreoffice-writer', '@docx-official') in markdown text, not actual shell commands. The skill contains no executable code.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains only documentation text listing skill names. No backtick syntax for shell execution exists - only @references to other skills.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at 16 locations. This is a misinterpretation of text strings like 'odf', 'docx', 'password' appearing in documentation. No cryptographic code exists.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The markdown file contains only documentation text. The scanner misinterprets file format extensions and common words as crypto patterns.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Network reconnaissance' at line 78. This is the skill name 'libreoffice-impress', not network code.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 78 contains the skill reference '@libreoffice-impress' which the scanner incorrectly matched as a network pattern.

content.value_statement: This skill provides a comprehensive workflow for creating, automating, and converting office documents using LibreOffice and Microsoft Office tools. It enables developers and content creators to programmatically generate documents, spreadsheets, and presentations without manual intervention.
actual_capabilities: Create ODT and DOCX documents using skill invocations
Automate ODS and XLSX spreadsheet generation with formulas and charts
Generate ODP and PPTX presentations from data templates
Convert between office document formats (ODT, DOCX, PDF, etc.)
Perform mail merge and bulk document generation
Integrate with database sources for automated reporting
limitations: This is a workflow guidance skill - actual document creation depends on invoked skills being installed
Requires LibreOffice or Microsoft Office installed on the system
Complex formatting may require additional skill customization
Some advanced features may need platform-specific configuration

use_cases.title: Automated Report Generation
use_cases.description: Generate weekly or monthly reports by automating spreadsheet creation with imported data, applying formulas, and exporting to PDF.

use_cases.title: Bulk Document Creation
use_cases.description: Create personalized documents for multiple recipients using mail merge, such as generating invoices or personalized letters.
use_cases.target_user: Administrative staff and small business owners

use_cases.title: Presentation from Data
use_cases.description: Transform data from spreadsheets into professional presentations with charts and formatted slides for stakeholder meetings.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Document Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @libreoffice-writer to create an ODT document with a professional template that includes header, footer, and styled paragraphs.

prompt_templates.title: Spreadsheet with Charts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @xlsx-official to create an Excel spreadsheet with sales data, apply formulas for totals, and generate a bar chart visualization.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a data report with visualizations

prompt_templates.title: Presentation Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @pptx-official to create a 10-slide presentation from the quarterly sales data, including title slide, agenda, charts, and conclusion.

prompt_templates.title: Format Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @libreoffice-writer to convert the document from DOCX to PDF format while preserving all formatting and images.

output_examples.output: A complete ODT document with sales data tables, calculated totals, formatted headers, and professional styling ready for distribution.
Multiple personalized PDF invoices generated from a spreadsheet data source, each with client-specific details and calculated amounts.

output_examples.input: Convert legacy documents to modern format
output_examples.output: Batch-converted documents from old formats to DOCX or ODT with preserved formatting and embedded images.

best_practices: Use consistent templates across all document types for professional appearance
Test automation workflows with sample data before production use
Keep skill invocations specific - specify exact formats and desired outputs
anti_patterns: Assuming all Office applications are available without checking system dependencies
Creating complex nested workflows without breaking them into testable phases
Ignoring error handling for format conversion failures

faq.question: Do I need Microsoft Office installed to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. The skill supports both LibreOffice (free, open source) and Microsoft Office. You can choose whichever is available on your system.

faq.question: Can this skill create password-protected documents?
faq.answer: Yes. When using @docx-official or @libreoffice-writer, you can specify password protection in the document generation parameters.

faq.question: What formats can be converted using this workflow?
faq.answer: Common conversions include: DOCX to ODT, ODT to PDF, XLSX to ODS, PPTX to ODP, and any to PDF format.

faq.question: Can I generate documents from database queries?
faq.answer: Yes. Phase 7 (Database Integration) covers connecting to databases and generating formatted reports from query results.

faq.question: How do I handle document generation errors?
faq.answer: Include error handling in your workflow. Check file existence, verify format compatibility, and validate output files after generation.

faq.question: Is batch processing supported for large document sets?
faq.answer: Yes. Phase 4 (Format Conversion) specifically addresses batch processing for converting or generating multiple files efficiently.
